patches.hypothesis: The method should throw a ClassCastException when a non-comparable object is added after an integer, but it currently throws an IllegalArgumentException. The fix involves changing the exception type to match the expected behavior in the test.
patches.changes:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw new ClassCastException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
patches.diff:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw new ClassCastException("Value not comparable to existing values.");

patches.hypothesis: The bug arises because the method catches a ClassCastException and throws an IllegalArgumentException instead, which does not match the expected behavior of the test. The test expects a ClassCastException to be thrown when adding non-comparable values.
patches.changes:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw ex;
patches.diff:
- throw new IllegalArgumentException("Value not comparable to existing values.");
+ throw ex;
